mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending and trigger negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline by at least 15% in out-of-sample Sharpe ratio over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without price pressures, generating positive monthly equity returns as firms expand output profitably.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial production rises alongside CPIAUCSL increases above 0.3% monthly, invalidating the low-inflation regime assumption.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently above 2.0 percent annualized confirms sustained economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and yields positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.5 percent triggers financial condition tightening that compresses equity multiples and forces negative quarterly market returns as borrowing costs rise.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in 70% of periods where the federal funds rate stays above 2.5 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and drives positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth decouples from unemployment declines, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak housing starts combined with stagnant personal consumption expenditures indicate a demand shortfall that forces corporate earnings revisions downward and produces negative weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances where both housing starts and consumption expenditures show simultaneous weakness.
